reasoning: According to an October 21, 2025 Reuters poll, 98% of economists (115 out of 117) expect the Fed to cut interest rates by 25 basis points at the October 28-29 meeting. Fed Governor Christopher Waller publicly endorsed a 25 bps reduction on October 16. The September Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) also implied a path of a 25 bps cut in October and another in December. Additionally, a government shutdown has suspended new economic data collection from the Labor and Commerce departments, making it highly unlikely that last-minute data would derail the strongly telegraphed 25 bps cut. A surprise pause or a larger 50 bps cut seem extremely improbable this close to the meeting given the strong consensus.
batch_queries: Fed decreases interest rates by 25 bps after October 2025 meeting?
